title: Goniolimon besserianum
intro: Goniolimon besserianum, also known as Besser's lemongrass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its lemon-scented flowers and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniolimon besserianum include plumbagin, a potent antioxidant, as well as other phenolic compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Goniolimon besserianum is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used to make ointments and salves for topical application.
taxonomy: Goniolimon besserianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Goniolimon, which is a group of plants commonly known as limonium or sea lavenders. The classification of Goniolimon besserianum can be broken down further into the following: kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Plumbaginales, family Plumbaginaceae, and genus Goniolimon. This classification system is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Understanding the classification of Goniolimon besserianum can provide insights into its biology, ecology, and potential uses.

preparations: Goniolimon besserianum is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Central Asia.
